assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and therefore can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup receivable can be considered a monetary asset whose foreign currency risk may be designated as a hedged item in a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a separate hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities when the hedged risk creates variability in cash flows affecting profit or loss.
